metric_name: NT Server ISV launch event attendance
metric_value: More than 5,000 ISVs attended Microsoft's initial NT Server software developers' conference in 1992; ~60,000 developer toolkits shipped by end of 1992

metric_name: NT Server 4.0 domain scale
metric_value: Up to 40,000 users in a single domain; up to 500 trusted domains depending on server configuration

metric_name: IIS 2.0 performance vs. IIS 1.0
metric_value: Microsoft claims IIS 2.0 is 40% faster than version 1.0; bundled with NT Server NOS installation
notes: Vendor claim; not independently benchmarked by Aberdeen.

metric_name: NT Server 4.0 hardware architecture support
metric_value: Versions for Intel, MIPS, Digital Alpha, PowerPC (IBM, Motorola, Groupe Bull); SMP support up to 32 processors
notes: Broad RISC support distinguished NT from competitors.

metric_name: LDAP adoption status in 1996
metric_value: Developed at University of Michigan as X.500 directory client; adopted as standard by major networking vendors in 1996; slated as core NT Server 5.0 directory technology

metric_name: NT Server 4.0 vs. 3.51 file/printer sharing performance on 100MB network
metric_value: NT 4.0 vs. 3.51: network frames -32%, bytes written to disk -27%, interrupts -50%, server code path -33%, improved cache flushing

metric_name: NT 4.0 application server performance improvements
metric_value: ~30% reduction in resource contention; 35% fewer resource allocations; new Pentium/PentiumPro optimizations; new MP communication protocol

metric_name: Microsoft NT Server four key themes
metric_value: 1) Convenience and compatibility, 2) Integrated Internet/intranet capabilities, 3) Network integration, 4) Performance enhancements

metric_name: Novell NetWare competitive position vs. NT Server
metric_value: Main competitor; NT Server now provides NTDS that manages NetWare 2.x and 3.x servers (not 4.x); NT encroaching on NetWare territory via wizard tools and departmental deployment model

metric_name: Aberdeen NT Server market assessment
metric_value: Aberdeen has not yet found a company or agency not in some stage of implementing NT Server; all interviews over past year indicate major increase in installations in 1997

metric_name: Aberdeen 'IBM of the 1990s' characterization
metric_value: Microsoft has become the IBM of the 1990s — a 'safe' bet because of marketing clout, ability to attract key ISVs, and persistence in improving core products
notes: Notably ironic framing given IBM's decline narrative at the time.

metric_name: Prediction: NT 4.0 heavy workhorse for database/application servers
metric_value: Within a year of its introduction, Aberdeen expects NT 4.0 to be seen as a heavy workhorse as a database and application server at the workgroup and departmental level

metric_name: Prediction: NT will displace NetWare in enterprise deployments
metric_value: Most new NT Server sales will be into new environments or at expense of OS/2, NetWare, and SCO competitors; NT staying in the race for long-haul keeping pressure on all competitors

metric_name: Prediction: NT 5.0 LDAP will solve enterprise directory gap
metric_value: Until NT Server 5.0 with LDAP and Exchange integration ships, NT missing essential interoperability; then single directory will overcome biggest obstacle to enterprise-wide deployment
notes: NT Server 5.0 shipped as Windows 2000 Server in February 2000.

metric_name: Outcome: NT Server line dominance as predicted
metric_value: Windows NT 4.0 succeeded by Windows 2000 Server (Feb 2000) and Windows Server 2003; dominated enterprise server market throughout late 1990s and 2000s; still active as Windows Server 2025 as of 2026
notes: Source: Windows NT Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Windows_NT); Microsoft Community Hub 30 Years of Windows Server. Aberdeen prediction proved completely accurate.

metric_name: Outcome: Novell NetWare decline and acquisition
metric_value: NetWare lost dominant NOS position to Windows NT by 1999; Novell acquired by Attachmate 2011 for $2.2B; NetWare effectively obsolete; Novell technologies eventually absorbed into OpenText (2023)
notes: Source: Novell Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Novell). Aberdeen's prediction of NetWare displacement was entirely accurate.

metric_name: Outcome: Windows 2000 Active Directory with LDAP
metric_value: Windows 2000 Server (released February 2000) shipped with Active Directory using LDAP as core directory technology as Aberdeen predicted
notes: Source: Windows Server Wikipedia. Aberdeen's prediction about NT 5.0/LDAP proved accurate; shipped on schedule.

metric_name: NT 4.0 reason for customer choice: Ease of deployment
metric_value: Ease of set-up, deployment, management, and use — identified by Microsoft focus groups as top customer reason for choosing NT

metric_name: NT 4.0 reason for customer choice: Compatibility
metric_value: Compatibility with existing systems — identified by Microsoft focus groups as customer reason for choosing NT

metric_name: NT 4.0 reason for customer choice: Single OS for multiple roles
metric_value: Ability to use same OS for application servers, file servers, and communication servers

metric_name: NT 4.0 reason for customer choice: Built-in web server
metric_value: Suitability as Internet platform because of built-in Web server (IIS)
